behavior_summary: A stationary 'fire field' item that: (a) shows the short description "fire field" when the player inspects it (U8P_FIREFELD::look), (b) runs a recurring animation/process that advances frames, spawns a persistent pentagram SFX loop if needed, detects overlapping NPCs and may damage them on specific frames (U8P_FIREFELD::anim), and (c) reacts to incoming hits/interactions by identifying a source/overlapping entity and potentially playing SFX and applying damage back to that entity (U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit).

Damage application is conditional: overlaps and isNpc checks filter valid targets, behavior is gated by local flags to avoid repeated effects, some type-specific cases (e.g., type == 0x60) trigger alternate SFX and gating behavior, and damage uses a random range for variability.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIREFELD::look is a simple process that immediately barks the text "fire field" and suspends (inspect/description path).

U8P_FIREFELD::anim loops the item's animation frame, performing per-frame logic: at certain frames it spawns a pentagram SFX loop (only if Kernel::getNumProcesses indicates none), collects overlapping NPCs into a local target list, and on impact frames plays SFX and calls Item::receiveHit for each target using a direction computed toward the target and a random damage amount.

U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit takes a parameter (attacker/handle). If non-zero it stores that handle, checks overlaps and NPC-ness, inspects the target type and current frame, and conditionally plays SFX and calls Item::receiveHit back at the overlapping NPC. Local gate flags are set/cleared to prevent repeated immediate reapplication.
No global flags are read or written by these scripts — state is managed via local/process variables and spawned processes.
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings of type constants (e.g., 0x60) used in type-special-case branches — the script plays a different SFX and clears a gate for that type, but the target identity is inferred only from the numeric constant.

Precise timing and scheduling of U8P_FIREFELD::anim relative to game ticks (how often damage windows occur and how long the pentagram SFX loop persists) — inferred from frame checks but exact cadence is unknown.

Whether the local 'menu/list' collected in U8P_FIREFELD::anim (local3) is used elsewhere by other systems or only within the immediate U8P_FIREFELD::anim loop — the script builds the list but higher-level use beyond immediate iteration is not explicit.

main_function.name: U8P_FIREFELD::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text when the player examines the fire field (display "fire field").
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_FIREFELD::look/inspect event for this item (process type 0x0208).
Runs when the player inspects or queries the fire field object in the world.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit
function_map.role: react-to-hit / retaliate / play SFX and optionally call Item::receiveHit on overlapping attacker/target

function_map.name: U8P_FIREFELD::anim
function_map.role: recurring animation/process: advance frames, spawn persistent SFX loop, detect overlapping NPCs and apply periodic damage on specific frames

external_calls.purpose: Read and advance/wrap the fire field's animation frame to drive per-frame effects and looping.
Check whether a pentagram SFX/process is already running for this item to avoid spawning duplicates.
Spawn the PENT::pentagramSFXLoop process attached to the item when appropriate frames occur and no duplicate exists.
Persistent pentagram sound/visual effect associated with the fire field.
Detect entities overlapping the fire field so they can be targeted for SFX/damage.
Filter overlapping entities to NPCs before adding them to the local target list and applying damage.
Collect overlapping NPCs into a local list/menu (local3) for subsequent per-target processing within the U8P_FIREFELD::anim loop.
Play impact or special-case sound effects on specific frames or when hitting certain types.
Generate a random damage amount used when calling Item::receiveHit.
Compute direction from the fire field to the target for Item::receiveHit's direction parameter.
Apply damage to overlapping NPCs with computed damage and direction.
Confirm the incoming parameter (attacker/handle) overlaps the fire field before retaliating or playing SFX.
Ensure the overlapping handle refers to an NPC before applying type-specific logic or damage.
Check the target's type to apply special-case behavior (e.g., type == 0x60 triggers a specific SFX and gating).
Read this item's frame to restrict retaliatory damage/SFX to early frames (frame <= 0x09 inferred).
Play a hit/impact sound effect when conditions are met.
Choose randomized damage amount when calling Item::receiveHit back at the attacker/overlapping NPC.
Compute direction toward the overlapping attacker to pass into Item::receiveHit.
Apply retaliatory damage to the overlapping attacker/target.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: A stationary 'fire field' item that: (a) shows the short description "fire field" when the player inspects it (U8P_FIREFELD::look), (b) runs a recurring animation/process that advances frames, spawns a persistent pentagram SFX loop if needed, detects overlapping NPCs and may damage them on specific frames (U8P_FIREFELD::anim), and (c) reacts to incoming hits/interactions by identifying a source/overlapping entity and potentially playing SFX and applying damage back to that entity (U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit).
